Title: Arabidopsis transcription factor TCP4 represses chlorophyll biosynthesis to prevent petal greening

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in the chlorophyll biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor of chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for chlorophyll accumulation in petals; directly repressed by TCP4 in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in green petal tissues, including wild-type proximal regions and tcp2/3/4/5/10/13/17 distal green regions; lower in white wild-type distal petal tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in green proximal petals and tcp mutant distal green petals; reduced in wild-type distal white petals
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting pigment; causes green petal coloration when retained
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petal proximal region in wild type; proximal and distal petal regions in tcp2/3/4/5/10/13/17 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PORB expression is associated with chlorophyll accumulation and green petals; repression of PORB contributes to white distal petals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; TCP4 ChIP-seq; ChIP-PCR; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; petal proximal versus distal region
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA792926; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE115589
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering green-flower traits by relieving TCP4-mediated repression or promoting white petals by strengthening repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a direct TCP4 target through promoter binding to TCP4-binding motif GGACCA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORB expression was lower in white distal wild-type petals and higher in green petal tissues. 2. TCP4 bound directly to the PORB promoter by ChIP-seq, ChIP-PCR, and EMSA. 3. Repression of PORB by TCP4 contributes to reduced chlorophyll biosynthesis and prevention of petal greening.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Vinyl reduction step in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces divinyl chlorophyll intermediates to monovinyl chlorophyll intermediates during chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in petal chlorophyll biosynthesis supported by direct TCP4 repression in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in green petal tissues and reduced in white distal wild-type petal tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in wild-type proximal green tissue and tcp mutant distal green tissue; reduced in wild-type distal white tissue
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigment and determinant of green petal color
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petal proximal region in wild type; distal and proximal petal regions in tcp mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated DVR expression correlates with chlorophyll accumulation and petal greening; TCP4-mediated repression contributes to white distal petals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; TCP4 ChIP-seq; ChIP-PCR; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; distal versus proximal petal patterning
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA792926; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE115589
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate chlorophyll retention or suppression in ornamental petals
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct TCP4 target with promoter TCP4-binding motif GGACCA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DVR was repressed in white distal petals and elevated in green petal tissues. 2. TCP4 directly bound the DVR promoter. 3. TCP4 repression of DVR helps reduce chlorophyll biosynthesis and prevent petal greening.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses PORB, DVR, and SOC1 expression by binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chlorophyll biosynthesis and chloroplast-to-leucoplast conversion during petal development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigment; retention causes green petal coloration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Proximal region of wild-type petals; both proximal and distal regions in tcp septuple mutant petals
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Prevents petal greening by repressing chlorophyll biosynthesis, causing distal petals to become white
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key CIN-like TCP required for green-to-white shift in petals; TCP4 genomic complementation fully rescued the green petal phenotype of the septuple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in distal petal regions from flower stages 8/9 to 12, strongest around stages 10–12; absent from proximal green petal region; fades by stage 13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly distal petal expression corresponding to white petal region
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TCP4 native promoter (2821 bp or 2818 bp), developmental/tissue-specific; 35S promoter used for ChIP-related transgene expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage and distal petal positional patterning
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; proximal versus distal petal region
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Septuple mutant analysis; genomic complementation with TCP4pro-TCP4-FLAG; GUS reporter; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; chlorophyll measurement; microscopy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter petal morphology and development because CIN-like TCPs also regulate petal growth and shape
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA792926; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE115589
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for breeding green ornamental flowers by weakening TCP4 activity, or for maintaining white distal petal pattern by enhancing TCP4 action
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among CIN-like TCPs, TCP4 had a stronger role than TCP10 in rescuing petal greening; functions redundantly/additively with TCP2/3/5/10/13/17
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tcp2/3/4/5/10/13/17 petals remained green because chlorophyll accumulated and chloroplast-to-leucoplast conversion failed in distal petals. 2. TCP4 expression in distal petals matched the white petal domain and developmental timing of color conversion. 3. TCP4 directly repressed PORB, DVR, and SOC1, thereby suppressing chlorophyll biosynthesis and preventing petal greening.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Chlorophyll biosynthesis-related genes (positive regulator, as described in prior work and inferred in this study)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes chlorophyll biosynthesis-related gene expression; TCP4 directly represses SOC1 promoter activity/expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Positive regulation of chlorophyll biosynthesis contributing to petal greening
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigment and green coloration determinant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SOC1 expression promotes petal greening; reduced SOC1 expression is associated with white distal petals
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known positive regulator of petal greening; overexpression previously reported to produce green petals, and TCP4 repression of SOC1 is part of the anti-greening mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower in white distal wild-type petal tissue; higher in green proximal wild-type tissue and green distal tcp mutant tissue
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched in green petal tissues relative to white distal petals
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flower developmental stage; proximal versus distal petal tissue
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; TCP4 ChIP-seq; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; literature-supported overexpression phenotype cited in paper
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI BioProject PRJNA792926; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE115589
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target to modulate green petal traits without directly altering TCP4
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a direct TCP4 target and chlorophyll-promoting TF; however, the paper's primary experimental focus is TCP4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOC1 expression was lower in white distal petals and higher in green petal tissues. 2. TCP4 directly bound the SOC1 promoter. 3. TCP4 likely suppresses chlorophyll biosynthesis partly through repression of SOC1, a positive regulator of petal greening.
